full_prompt: Design a single-part, monolithic airplane bearing bracket per the aluminum-parts supplier / open-CAD platform Airplane Bearing Bracket Challenge (2014). The bracket supports a bearing pin connecting a fixed structure to a rotating accessory on a commercial aircraft. Objective: minimize mass while passing three independent static load cases at the bearing pin. Geometry: bounding envelope 150 mm x 100 mm x 150 mm (approximate challenge envelope). Top mounting face has 4x 6.35 mm (0.25 in) through-holes on a 60 mm x 40 mm rectangular pattern. Bearing pin hole at center-bottom, 15.875 mm (5/8 in) diameter, pin axis horizontal. Vertical offset from mounting plate to pin centerline 75 mm. Single monolithic body, no welds or fasteners; DfAM (Design for Additive Manufacturing) rules apply: all overhangs >= 45 deg, internal voids must include drain holes >= 2.5 mm. Material: PH 15-5 stainless steel (precipitation-hardened), E = 200 GPa, yield = 1000 MPa (145 ksi), ultimate = 1100 MPa (160 ksi), density = 7800 kg/m^3. Three challenge load cases applied at the pin hole (mounting face bolts fully fixed): LC1 Horizontal 1250 lbf (5,560 N) along engine axis; LC2 Inclined 1875 lbf (8,340 N) at 45 deg off-vertical; LC3 Vertical 2500 lbf (11,120 N) vertical up. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises under each case <= yield / 1.2 = 833 MPa (challenge stress limit); (R2) max displacement at pin center per case <= 0.5 mm; (R3) final mass <= 170 g with target >= 50% reduction vs published baseline 380 g (published challenge winners reached sub-170 g); (R4) linear buckling first-mode load factor under LC3 >= 2.5. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static x3) + linear buckling. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Topology optimization is required as outer loop. Deliverables: STEP AP242 + Parasolid, DfAM build-orientation recommendation, and a report listing mass in g, max von Mises per case in MPa, pin displacement per case in mm, first buckling load factor.
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic PH 15-5 stainless airplane bearing bracket per the aluminum-parts supplier open-CAD Airplane Bearing Bracket Challenge (2014). The bracket supports a bearing pin connecting a fixed structure to a rotating accessory on a commercial aircraft. Three challenge load cases are applied at the 15.875 mm (5/8 in) pin hole with the 4 x 6.35 mm top-flange bolt holes (60 x 40 mm pattern) fully fixed. Pin-center displacement <= 0.5 mm per case. DfAM rules apply (overhangs >= 45 deg; drain holes >= 2.5 mm in any internal voids). Topology optimization is required as the outer loop.

Envelope: bounding 150 x 100 x 150 mm; mounting-plate-to-pin vertical offset 75 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 horizontal along engine axis: 1250 lbf (5,560 N).
- LC2 inclined 45 deg off-vertical: 1875 lbf (8,340 N).
- LC3 vertical up: 2500 lbf (11,120 N).
- LC4 linear buckling under LC3, first-mode load factor >= 2.5.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 50% mass reduction vs published baseline is the scoring driver.

prompt.title: aluminum-parts supplier Airplane Bearing Bracket
prompt.brief: PH15-5 monolithic bracket, 3 challenge load cases (H 1250, incl 1875, V 2500 lbf), topology opt target <=170 g.

eval_coverage: aluminum-parts supplier three static load cases
